task_completion.description: Create required layers with correct colors
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the LibreCAD layer list (usually on the right side of the screen), there should be a layer named 'Irrigation_Zones' with a green color indicator, and a layer named 'Irrigation_Arms' with a red color indicator.

task_completion.description: Draw three concentric circles for the irrigation zones
task_completion.visual_evidence: Three concentric circles are visible in the main drawing area. They should be drawn in green, indicating they belong to the 'Irrigation_Zones' layer. The command line or coordinate display may show the center is at (200, 200) and radii are 50, 100, and 150.

task_completion.description: Draw four radial lines in cardinal directions
task_completion.visual_evidence: Four straight lines radiate from the center of the circles outwards to the edge of the largest circle, pointing exactly Up, Down, Left, and Right (North, South, West, East). They should be drawn in red, indicating they belong to the 'Irrigation_Arms' layer.

task_completion.description: Add text labels for the zones
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text labels 'Zone A', 'Zone B', and 'Zone C' are visible on the canvas, placed near their respective concentric circles.

task_completion.description: Save the drawing to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD window title bar displays 'irrigation_layout.dxf', or a save dialog shows the file being saved to the '/home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/' directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the LibreCAD GUI to create the drawing
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the LibreCAD interface (selecting tools, entering coordinates, managing layers) rather than writing a script in a text editor or terminal to generate the DXF file.

integrity.description: Agent actually drew the shapes instead of loading a pre-made file
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory should show the step-by-step construction of the drawing (e.g., creating layers first, then drawing circles, then lines, then text) rather than the completed drawing suddenly appearing.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a DXF file in LibreCAD with specific geometry: three concentric circles at center (200,200) with radii 50, 100, 150 on a green 'Irrigation_Zones' layer, and four radial lines in cardinal directions (East, North, West, South) on a red 'Irrigation_Arms' layer. Text labels 'Zone A', 'Zone B', and 'Zone C' must be added. The final file must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/irrigation_layout.dxf.
